rationale: Ground truth has no real concerns (Codex hit its limit, human just said 'lgtm'), so both reviews correctly land on approve/no-blocker verdicts and neither misses anything. But Y's cited line numbers (754, 1017, 1157, 1248, 1286, 1329, 2533, 2546, 2615) all check out exactly against the diff hunks, and Y surfaces one genuinely actionable, well-evidenced finding (the error-response path via `_create_speech_error_json_response` isn't directly test-covered) with a concrete suggested test and command. X's narrative is solid but its test-file line references (2638, 2651, 2720) are wrong by ~100 lines (actual lines are ~2533/2546/2615), and it cites an unverifiable upstream PR number (vllm#46022) and specific upstream line numbers that can't be checked from the diff, adding fabrication risk to an otherwise reasonable review.
